toolArgs.name: f3-mobile-audio
toolArgs.prompt:
## Task: Implement Phase F3 — Mobile Audio Handling

You are implementing Phase F3 of the Mobile Support & UI Refresh feature (`docs/features/mobile-and-ui-refresh.md`). Phases F1 (HUD simplification) and F2 (responsive layout) are already complete with 272 tests passing.

### Context

The existing audio capture session lives at `src/features/audio/input/session.ts`. It currently:
- Has `autoGainControl: false` hardcoded in `DEFAULT_AUDIO_CAPTURE_CONSTRAINTS`
- Creates an AudioContext but doesn't handle iOS Safari's gesture-gate requirement
- Has no visibility change recovery
- The `ensureRunning()` method already calls `audioContext.resume()` when suspended

The mic readiness flow is managed by the audio state in `src/features/audio/`.

### What to build

**1. `resumeOnGesture()` utility in `session.ts`**
- Add a `resumeOnGesture(audioContext: AudioContext): Promise<void>` function
- If the AudioContext state is `'suspended'`, attach a one-time `click`/`touchstart` listener to `document` that calls `audioContext.resume()`
- Returns a promise that resolves when the context is running
- If already running, resolve immediately
- This handles iOS Safari which requires a user gesture before the AudioContext can start

**2. Integrate gesture-based resume into `createAudioInputSession()`**
- After creating the AudioContext, check if it's suspended and call `resumeOnGesture()` if needed
- Update `ensureRunning()` to also use `resumeOnGesture()` as a fallback when `resume()` alone doesn't work (iOS)

**3. Make `autoGainControl` configurable with mobile-aware defaults**
- Add a `isMobileDevice()` helper (use `navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0` — not user agent sniffing)
- When creating the session, if no explicit `autoGainControl` override is provided, default to `true` on mobile and `false` on desktop
- Keep the existing override mechanism working so callers can still force a specific value

**4. Background-tab recovery via `visibilitychange`**
- In `createAudioInputSession()`, add a `visibilitychange` event listener
- When the tab becomes visible again (`document.visibilityState === 'visible'`), check if the AudioContext is suspended and re-resume it
- Clean up the listener in `close()`
- Consider adding a brief status indicator — the `AudioInputSession` interface could expose an optional callback or event for "audio resumed" status

**5. Update guidance copy for mobile**
- Look at how "unsupported" and "blocked" states are communicated to users in the HomeScreen or audio status components
- Update any user-facing strings to include mobile-specific instructions (e.g., "On iPhone, tap Allow when prompted" or "Make sure your browser tab is in the foreground")
- Look in `src/screens/HomeScreen/HomeScreen.tsx` and any audio status components for these strings

**6. Tests**
- Write unit tests for `resumeOnGesture()` — mock AudioContext states
- Test that `isMobileDevice()` correctly detects touch devices
- Test that `autoGainControl` defaults to `true` on mobile and `false` on desktop
- Test the `visibilitychange` recovery logic
- Ensure all existing audio tests still pass (regression)

### Requirements to satisfy
- FT-FR-10: AudioContext resumes on user gesture for iOS Safari
- FT-FR-11: On mobile, autoGainControl defaults to true
- FT-NF-03: No interference with 150ms pitch-to-feedback latency budget
- Feature PRD Section 13, Q5: Show brief "Audio resumed" indicator when recovering from suspension

### Existing conventions
- TypeScript strict mode
- Vitest for testing
- Privacy-first: no raw audio stored (SP-02/SP-06)
- Audio capture types in `src/features/audio/input/types.ts`
- Barrel exports in `src/features/audio/input/index.ts` and `src/features/audio/index.ts`

### Verification
After all changes, run:
1. `npm run typecheck`
2. `npm run test -- --run`
3. `npm run build`

All must pass. Report results including test count.

toolArgs.name: f4-tooltips
toolArgs.prompt:
## Task: Implement Phase F4 — First-Run Contextual Tooltips

You are implementing Phase F4 of the Mobile Support & UI Refresh feature (`docs/features/mobile-and-ui-refresh.md`). Phases F1 (HUD simplification), F2 (responsive layout), and F3 (mobile audio) are being completed in parallel.

### What to build

**1. Create `Tooltip` component at `src/shared/components/Tooltip.tsx`**
- Lightweight positioned tooltip component — NO external dependencies
- Props: `targetRef: React.RefObject<HTMLElement>`, `content: React.ReactNode`, `position: 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'`, `visible: boolean`, `onDismiss: () => void`
- Uses CSS absolute/fixed positioning relative to the target element
- Include a "Got it" dismiss button on each tooltip
- Must not obscure critical gameplay elements on mobile viewports (consider viewport size when positioning)
- Should be keyboard accessible (dismissible via Escape key)

**2. Create `useFirstRunTooltips` hook at `src/shared/hooks/useFirstRunTooltips.ts`**
- Tracks which tooltip groups the player has seen via localStorage key `viben:tooltips-seen`
- Interface: `{ shouldShow(groupId: string): boolean, dismiss(groupId: string): void, dismissAll(): void, currentTooltip: string | null, advance(): void }`
- Groups: `'prompt-card'`, `'stability-meter'`, `'rocket-feedback'`, `'moon-progress'`
- **Sequenced display**: Show one tooltip at a time in the order listed above. When one is dismissed, show the next.
- Include a "Skip all tips" option that calls `dismissAll()` to skip remaining tooltips
- Follow existing persistence conventions: the `viben:tooltips-seen` key should handle invalid/corrupt data gracefully (similar to how `viben:progression` works — see `src/shared/persistence/` for patterns)
- Export from `src/shared/hooks/index.ts` barrel

**3. Add tooltip triggers to GameScreen**
- In `src/screens/GameScreen/GameScreen.tsx`, add refs to key HUD elements: PromptFocusCard area, Stability HudMeter, RocketFlightCard area, Moon Progress HudMeter
- Render `<Tooltip>` components attached to these refs, controlled by `useFirstRunTooltips`
- Tooltip content should be brief and helpful:
  - Prompt card: "Sing the note shown here to control your rocket!"
  - Stability meter: "Keep your pitch steady to maintain stability"
  - Rocket feedback: "Watch the rocket's flame — it shows how well you're matching"
  - Moon progress: "This tracks your journey to the moon"
- Only show tooltips during the FIRST game run (not during pre-run or post-run states)
- Include a "Skip all tips" link/button visible on each tooltip

**4. Add tooltip CSS to `src/styles/global.css`**
- Add a clearly commented section: `/* ── F4: First-run Tooltips (FT-FR-12, FT-FR-13) ── */`
- Style the tooltip with the existing retro aesthetic (monospace font, glow effects)
- Semi-transparent dark background with retro border
- "Got it" button styled consistently with existing buttons
- "Skip all tips" as a subtle link
- Ensure tooltips are responsive — don't overflow on mobile viewports (320px minimum)
- Add `prefers-reduced-motion` guard for any tooltip entrance animations
- Use appropriate z-index so tooltips appear above game elements

**5. Tests**
- Test `useFirstRunTooltips`: initial state shows first tooltip, dismiss advances to next, dismissAll stops all, persistence in localStorage, handles corrupt localStorage data
- Test `Tooltip` component: renders when visible, doesn't render when not visible, calls onDismiss on "Got it" click, keyboard dismiss with Escape
- Ensure all existing tests still pass

### Requirements to satisfy
- FT-FR-12: First-time players see contextual tooltips on key HUD elements during first game run
- FT-FR-13: Tooltips are dismissible and don't reappear after dismissal (state persisted in localStorage)
- FT-NF-02: Tooltip animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
- FT-NF-03: Tooltip system doesn't interfere with 150ms latency budget
- FT-NF-05: `viben:tooltips-seen` follows existing persistence conventions
- FT-NF-06: CSS stays in global.css with clear section comments

### Existing conventions
- TypeScript strict mode
- CSS classes (not CSS modules) in `src/styles/global.css`
- Retro monospace HUD styling with `.retro-hud`, `.retro-glow` classes
- Vitest for testing, React Testing Library for component tests
- localStorage persistence patterns in `src/shared/persistence/` — examine for error recovery patterns
- Barrel exports in `src/shared/hooks/index.ts` (already has `useViewport`)
- The useViewport hook at `src/shared/hooks/useViewport.ts` is available if you need viewport awareness for tooltip positioning

### Verification
After all changes, run:
1. `npm run typecheck`
2. `npm run test -- --run`
3. `npm run build`

All must pass. Report results including test count.

toolArgs.new_str:
- `useViewport` hook uses `matchMedia` (not `resize` events) to stay in sync with CSS breakpoints

### Phase F3 — Completed Tasks

- [x] F3.1: Add `resumeOnGesture()` utility to `session.ts` for iOS Safari AudioContext gesture gate
- [x] F3.2: Integrate gesture-based AudioContext resume into `createAudioInputSession()` and `ensureRunning()`
- [x] F3.3: Add `isMobileDevice()` helper and mobile-aware `autoGainControl` defaults (true on mobile, false on desktop)
- [x] F3.4: Add background-tab recovery via `visibilitychange` with `onAudioResumed` callback
- [x] F3.5: Update HomeScreen guidance copy with mobile-specific instructions for blocked/unsupported/error states
- [x] F3.6: Add 17 unit tests for mobile audio features (`src/features/audio/input/session.test.ts`)

### Phase F3 — Verification

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `npm run typecheck` | ✅ Pass |
| `npm run test -- --run` | ✅ 313 passed, 0 failures |
| `npm run build` | ✅ Built successfully |

### Phase F3 — Notes
- `isMobileDevice()` uses `navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0` (not user-agent sniffing)
- `resumeOnGesture()` attaches one-shot `click`/`touchstart` listeners — no per-frame cost
- `visibilitychange` listener cleaned up in `close()`
- No changes to the `readFrame()` hot path — NF-01 latency budget preserved

### Phase F4 — Completed Tasks

- [x] F4.1: Create `Tooltip` component (`src/shared/components/Tooltip.tsx`) — viewport-aware positioning, keyboard accessible (Escape), "Got it" + "Skip all tips"
- [x] F4.2: Create `useFirstRunTooltips` hook (`src/shared/hooks/useFirstRunTooltips.ts`) — sequenced display, localStorage persistence, corrupt data recovery
- [x] F4.3: Add tooltip triggers to GameScreen — refs on PromptFocusCard, Stability meter, RocketFlightCard, Moon Progress meter
- [x] F4.4: Add tooltip CSS to `global.css` — retro aesthetic, responsive, `prefers-reduced-motion` guard, z-index 2000
- [x] F4.5: Add 24 tests — 13 for useFirstRunTooltips, 11 for Tooltip component

### Phase F4 — Verification

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `npm run typecheck` | ✅ Pass |
| `npm run test -- --run` | ✅ 313 passed (272 + 41 new across F3+F4), 0 failures |
| `npm run build` | ✅ Built successfully (402 KB JS, 20.5 KB CSS) |

### Phase F4 — Notes
- Tooltips show sequentially: prompt-card → stability-meter → rocket-feedback → moon-progress
- Only displayed during active gameplay (not pre-run or post-run)
- `viben:tooltips-seen` follows existing persistence conventions with graceful error recovery
- Tooltip component uses `aria-live="polite"` and auto-focuses dismiss button for screen readers

toolArgs.summary:
## ✅ Mobile Support & UI Refresh — All Phases Complete

All 4 feature phases executed successfully:

| Phase | Name | Agent | New Tests |
|-------|------|-------|-----------|
| F1 | HUD Simplification & Rocket Visual Feedback | `ui-hud-developer` | +16 |
| F2 | Responsive Layout & Touch Support | `ui-hud-developer` | +6 |
| F3 | Mobile Audio Handling | `audio-systems-engineer` | +17 |
| F4 | First-Run Contextual Tooltips | `ui-hud-developer` | +24 |

**Final verification**: 313 tests passing, clean typecheck, clean build (402 KB JS, 20.5 KB CSS)

### Key deliverables
- **F2**: Responsive CSS breakpoints (768px/480px), 44×44px touch targets, `useViewport` hook, portrait/landscape support
- **F3**: iOS Safari `resumeOnGesture()`, mobile-aware `autoGainControl`, `visibilitychange` tab recovery, mobile guidance copy
- **F4**: Sequenced tooltip system (prompt → stability → rocket → moon), `Tooltip` component with viewport-aware positioning, `useFirstRunTooltips` hook with localStorage persistence

All work committed on branch `feat/ui-mobile-suppot`. `docs/PROGRESS.md` updated.
